title: Short Introduction to BAMP
text: BAMP includes data example.  simulating APC data, see vignette(“simulation”, package=“bamp”).
code: data(apc) plot(cases[,1],type="l",ylim=range(cases), ylab="cases", xlab="year", main="cases per age group") for (i in 2:8)lines(cases[,i], col=i)

what: APC model with random walk first order prior
title: Short Introduction to BAMP
text: bamp() automatically performs check MCMC convergence using Gelman Rubin’s convergence diagnostic. can manually check convergence : Now look model results. includes estimates smoothing parameters deviance DIC: can plot main APC effects using point-wise quantiles:  quantiles possible:  models see vignette(“modeling”,package=“bamp”).
code: model1 <- bamp(cases, population, age="rw1", period="rw1", cohort="rw1",               periods_per_agegroup = 5) ## Note: Parallel execution failed (2 nodes produced errors; first error: there is no package called ‘bamp’); falling back to sequential. ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. ##  ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. ##  ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. ##  ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. ##  ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. ##  ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. ##  ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. ##  ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. ##  ## Acceptance rate low. Restarting iterations. checkConvergence(model1) ## [1] TRUE print(model1) ##  ##  Model: ## age (rw1)  - period (rw1)  - cohort (rw1) model ## Deviance:     231.30 ## pD:            37.04 ## DIC:          268.34 ##  ##  ##  Hyper parameters:                 5%           50%          95%          ## age                              0.355        0.924        1.949 ## period                          67.552      195.013      620.193 ## cohort                          34.500       58.736       96.781 ##  ##  ## Markov Chains convergence checked succesfully using Gelman's R (potential scale reduction factor). plot(model1) plot(model1, quantiles = c(0.025,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.975))

title: Short Introduction to BAMP
text: Using prior assumption random walk period cohort effect, one can predict cases upcoming years.  details see vignette(“prediction”,package=“bamp”).
code: pred <- predict_apc(object=model1, periods=3) m<-max(pred$pr[2,,]) plot(pred$pr[2,,8],type="l", ylab="probability", xlab="year", ylim=c(0,m)) for (i in 7:1)   lines(pred$pr[2,,i],col=8-i) legend(1,m,col=8:1,legend=paste("Age group",1:8),lwd=2,cex=0.6) lines(c(10.5,10.5),c(0,1),lty=2)

what: APC model with random walk first order prior
title: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Modeling
text: bamp() automatically performs check MCMC convergence using Gelman Rubin’s convergence diagnostic. can manually check convergence : Now look model results. includes estimates smoothing parameters deviance DIC: can plot main APC effects using point-wise quantiles:  quantiles possible:
code: model1 <- bamp(cases, population, age="rw1", period="rw1", cohort="rw1",               periods_per_agegroup = 5) checkConvergence(model1) ## [1] TRUE print(model1) ##  ##  Model: ## age (rw1)  - period (rw1)  - cohort (rw1) model ## Deviance:     231.44 ## pD:            36.91 ## DIC:          268.35 ##  ##  ##  Hyper parameters:                 5%           50%          95%          ## age                              0.353        0.939        2.008 ## period                          68.286      197.863      618.072 ## cohort                          34.381       59.508       96.741 ##  ##  ## Markov Chains convergence checked succesfully using Gelman's R (potential scale reduction factor). plot(model1) plot(model1, quantiles = c(0.025,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.975))

what: APC model with random walk second order prior
title: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Modeling
code: model2 <- bamp(cases, population, age="rw2", period="rw2", cohort="rw2",               periods_per_agegroup = 5,               mcmc.options=list("number_of_iterations"=200000, "burn_in"=100000, "step"=50, "tuning"=500),               hyperpar=list("age"=c(1,.5), "period"=c(1,0.05), "cohort"=c(1,0.05))) checkConvergence(model2) ## [1] TRUE print(model2) ##  ##  Model: ## age (rw2)  - period (rw2)  - cohort (rw2) model ## Deviance:     234.27 ## pD:            36.77 ## DIC:          271.04 ##  ##  ##  Hyper parameters:                 5%           50%          95%          ## age                              1.054        2.863        6.478 ## period                          16.626       41.725       89.427 ## cohort                          23.759       45.271       82.147 ##  ##  ## Markov Chains convergence checked succesfully using Gelman's R (potential scale reduction factor). plot(model2) model3<-bamp(cases, population, age="rw1", period=" ", cohort="rw2",               periods_per_agegroup = 5) ## hain. Please check for convergence using checkConvergence() and maybe change your model settings (maybe add overdispersion). checkConvergence(model3) ## [1] TRUE print(model3) ##  ##  Model: ## age (rw1) cohort (rw2) model ## Deviance:     276.65 ## pD:            30.23 ## DIC:          306.88 ##  ##  ##  Hyper parameters:                 5%           50%          95%          ## age                              0.278        0.737        1.469 ## cohort                          38.698       74.630      140.661 ##  ##  ## Markov Chains convergence checked succesfully using Gelman's R (potential scale reduction factor). plot(model3) (model4<-bamp(cases, population, age="rw1", period="rw1", cohort="rw1",              cohort_covariate = cov_c, periods_per_agegroup = 5)) plot(model4) (model5<-bamp(cases, population, age="rw1", period="rw1", cohort="rw1",              period_covariate = cov_p, periods_per_agegroup = 5)) plot(model5)

title: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Prediction
text: Using prior assumption random walk period cohort effect, one can predict cases upcoming years. , use included data example.  us nine years predict last year. Plot predicted cases credible intervals true data  Plot period cohort effects including prediction year 10.
code: data(apc) plot(cases[,1],type="l",ylim=range(cases), ylab="cases", xlab="year", main="cases per age group") for (i in 2:8)lines(cases[,i], col=i) model0 <- bamp(cases[-10,], population[-10,], age="rw1", period="rw1", cohort="rw1",               periods_per_agegroup = 5) model0<-predict_apc(object=model0, periods=1, population=population, update = TRUE) ts.plot(t(model0$predicted$cases_period), lty=c(2,1,2)) points(apply(cases,1,sum), pch=19) ts.plot(t(model0$predicted$period), lty=c(2,1,2)) ts.plot(t(model0$predicted$cohort), lty=c(2,1,2))

title: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Modeling and Prediction
text: BAMP software package analyze incidence mortality data Lexis diagram, using Bayesian version age-period-cohort model. models described , e.g., Berzuini Clayton (1994), Besag, J.E., P.J. Green, D.M. Higdon K.L. Mengersen (1995) Knorr-Held Rainer (2001). pixel Lexis diagram (specific age group specific period) data must available number persons risk (population number) number disease cases (typically cancer incidence mortality). hierarchical model assumed binomial model first-stage. smoothing priors age, period cohort parameters random walks first second order (RW1 RW2) available. BAMP also allows drop one latent components, example drop cohort effect analyze age-period model. Additional unstructured prior distributions assumed pixel Lexis diagram. Note nonidentifiability likelihood APC-model, see Clayton Schifflers (1987), indices problems interpreting latent effects. RW1 model, parameters (weakly) identifiable. BAMP several features described detailed Knorr-Held Rainer (2001): data need grid, example period can one year intervals age group five year intervals. BAMP allows prediction future number cases BAMP allows retrospective prediction model checking Additionally model described Knorr-Held Rainer (2001), BAMP can handle - AP AC models - models without global heterogenity parameter (overdispersion) - models additional age, period /cohort heterogenity - including covariates (still development) Detail feature can found Schmid (2004 - German) graphical routines available order plot estimated age, period cohort effects (RW1 model) compare observed fitted rates predict rates assess “significance” unstructured parameters. helps identify variation data, supported age, period cohort parameters.

title: Simulate from age-period-cohort model — apcSimulate
text: intercept Intercept age Vector effect age groups period Vector effects periods cohort Vector effect cohorts periods_per_agegroup Periods per age group population Population number. Either matrix scalar.

title: Simulate from age-period-cohort model — apcSimulate
text: List number cases (matrix) population numbers (matrix).

title: Simulate from age-period-cohort model — apcSimulate
code: age=sqrt(seq(5,0,length=10)); age<-1-age-mean(age) period=15:1; period[8:15]<-8:15; period<-period/6; period<-period-mean(period) periods_per_agegroup=5; number_of_cohorts <- periods_per_agegroup*(10-1)+15 cohort<-rep(0,60); cohort[1:10]<-10:1; cohort[41:60]<- -(1:20)/2; cohort<-cohort/10; cohort<-cohort-mean(cohort) simdata<-apcSimulate(-5, age, period, cohort, periods_per_agegroup, 1e6) par(mfrow=c(3,1)) plot(age, type="l") plot(period, type="l") plot(cohort, type="l")  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ simmod <- bamp(cases = simdata$cases, population = simdata$population, age = "rw1",  period = "rw1", cohort = "rw1", periods_per_agegroup =periods_per_agegroup) plot(simmod) } # }

what: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Modeling and Prediction (bamp) — bamp
title: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Modeling and Prediction (bamp) — bamp
text: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Modeling analyze incidence mortality data Lexis diagram. pixel Lexis diagram (specific age group specific period) data must available number persons risk (population number) number disease cases (typically cancer incidence mortality). hierarchical model assumed binomial model first-stage. smoothing priors age, period cohort parameters random walks first second order (RW1 RW2) available. Deviance information criterion effective number parameters computed model comparison. Note non-identifiability likelihood APC-model, see e.g. Clayton Schifflers (1987, DOI:10.1002/sim.4780060406), indices problems interpreting latent effects. RW1 model, parameters (weakly) identifiable. Period age groups need grid, example periods can one year intervals age groups five year intervals. Additionally model described Knorr-Held Rainer (2001, DOI:10.1093/biostatistics/2.1.109), bamp can handle models without global heterogeneity parameter (overdispersion), models additional age, period /cohort heterogeneity, additional covariates.

title: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Modeling and Prediction (bamp) — bamp
code: bamp(   cases,   population,   age,   period,   cohort,   overdisp = FALSE,   period_covariate = NULL,   cohort_covariate = NULL,   periods_per_agegroup,   mcmc.options = list(number_of_iterations = 1e+05, burn_in = 50000, step = 50, tuning =     500),   hyperpar = list(age = c(1, 0.5), period = c(1, 5e-04), cohort = c(1, 5e-04), overdisp =     c(1, 0.05)),   dic = TRUE,   parallel = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Bayesian Age-Period-Cohort Modeling and Prediction (bamp) — bamp
text: cases number cases population population number age prior age groups ("rw1", "rw2", "rw1+het", "rw2+het", " ") period prior periods ("rw1", "rw2", "rw1+het", "rw2+het", " ") cohort prior cohorts ("rw1", "rw2", "rw1+het", "rw2+het", " ") overdisp logical, add overdispersion model period_covariate covariate period cohort_covariate covariate cohort periods_per_agegroup periods per age group mcmc.options list options MCMC. burn_in: number iterations used burnin beginning algorithm, iterations removed. step: Step size, example default 50, every 50th iterations stored. tuning: number iterations automatic tuning. Depending model, MCMC algorithm tune certain parameters efficient MCMC chains. tuning, algorithm restarted. hyperpar list hyper parameters. hyper prior precision (inverse variance) random walk priors Gamma distribution parameters \(\) \(b\); expected value \(/b\), variance \(/b^2\). Weak hyper parameters suggested, defaults \(=1, b=0.5\) age, \(=1, b=0.0005\) period cohort effects \(=1, b=0.05\) overdispersion (added). recommended choose hyper priors depending model, particular order random walk. dic logical. true. DIC computed parallel logical, computation done parallel. uses parallel package, allow parallel computing Windows. verbose verbose mode

title: Prediction for age-period-cohort models — predict_apc
text: Prediction rates , possible, cases Bayesian age-period-cohort model using prior assumptions (random walks) model estimated variance random walk. example, random walk first order (rw1) period effect predicts constant effects future periods plus noise.

title: bamp 2.2.0
text: Effects (age, period, cohort) now computed automatically inside bamp() stored returned object (model$effects), separate call effects.apc() longer needed default median summary. Fixed bug effects.apc(): cache check looking x$effect (singular) instead x$effects (plural), referenced undefined variable cache condition. MCMC chains now use warm starts: restart previous sample values used initial values, reducing burn-time automatic convergence checking. First-run behaviour unchanged. Fixed bug cohort heterogeneity hyperparameter check (cohort="rw2+het" recognized correctly). Fixed typo period_covariate handling silently prevented vector coercion. Fixed checkConvergence(): cohort convergence check used age hyperparameter instead cohort hyperparameter. Fixed hyperparameter display checkConvergence() info output. Fixed memory leaks C++ MCMC code (raw pointer allocations per iteration). Fixed GetRNGstate/PutRNGstate pairing random number generation. Added Cholesky decomposition error check informative message. Replaced fork-based mclapply socket-based makeCluster/parLapply reliable parallel execution platforms, including macOS GUI environments (RStudio). Falls back sequential cluster setup fails. MCMC chains fail due numerical errors now discarded cleanly instead continuing corrupted values. Cholesky decomposition now recovers non-positive-definite precision matrices (common RW2 priors tuning) adding increasing diagonal regularization retrying, instead aborting chain. Added unit tests. Updated CITATION use bibentry().
